title: Easter Zunday
content:
Last Easter Jim put on his blue
Frock cwoat, the vuï¿½st time-vier new;
Wiï¿½ yollow buttons all oï¿½ brass,
That glitterï¿½d in the zun likï¿½ glass;
Anï¿½ pokï¿½d ï¿½ithin the button-hole
A tutty heï¿½d a-beggï¿½d or stole.
A span-new wes-coï¿½t, too, he wore,
Wiï¿½ yellow stripes all down avore;
Anï¿½ tied his breechesï¿½ lags below
The knee, wiï¿½ ribbon in a bow;
Anï¿½ drowï¿½d his kitty-boots azide,
Anï¿½ put his laggens on, anï¿½ tied
His shoes wiï¿½ strings two vingers wide,
Because ï¿½twer Easter Zunday.

Anï¿½ after mornen church wer out
He come back hwome, anï¿½ strollï¿½d about
All down the vields, anï¿½ drough the leane,
Wiï¿½ sister Kit anï¿½ cousin Jeane,
A-turnen proudly to their view
His yollow breast anï¿½ back oï¿½ blue.
The lambs did play, the grounds wer green,
The trees did bud, the zun did sheen;
The lark did zing below the sky,
Anï¿½ roads wer all a-blown so dry,
As if the zummer wer begun;
Anï¿½ he had sich a bit oï¿½ fun!
He meade the maidens squeal anï¿½ run,
Because ï¿½twer Easter Zunday.
meaning:
About: William Barnes (22nd February 1801 - 7th October 1886) was an English writer, poet, minister, and philologist. 
He wrote over 800 poems, some in Dorset dialect and much other work including a comprehensive English grammar quoting from more than 70 different languages.
